title: asystasia drake-brockmanii var. typica
intro: Asystasia drake-brockmanii var. typica is a plant species, commonly known as the 'Wolly Wolly' or 'Wild Basil', native to Western Australia. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits associated with Asystasia drake-brockmanii var. typica include reducing inflammation, relieving digestive issues, and supporting the immune system. The plant is traditionally used in herbal preparations, such as teas and infusions, which can be made by steeping the leaves in hot water or combining them with other herbs.
